commit.message:
Add FEP-5711 collection inverse properties

Add FEP-5711 inverse properties to the bot actor's outbox and
followers collections by patching ActivityPub collection responses
in BotImpl.fetch().

Add fetch-level regression coverage for actor, collection root,
and paged collection responses, and document the change in the
bot concept docs and changelog.

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Upgrade BotKit to Fedify 2.0

Align BotKit with Fedify 2.0.3 and its modular package split.
Move vocab and runtime imports to @fedify/vocab,
@fedify/vocab-runtime, and @fedify/denokv.

Update the public API to match Fedify 2.0 by replacing
LanguageTag-based surfaces with Intl.Locale, using plain strings
for software.version, and removing parseSemVer(), SemVer,
LanguageTag, and parseLanguageTag() exports.

Refresh examples, docs, and CHANGES.md to reflect the new
package paths and API changes. Update lockfiles and manifests
for the new dependency graph.

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: botkit
repository.description: A framework for creating ActivityPub bots

commit.message:
fix(core): backport upstream orchestration and workflow fixes

Summary:
- backport symlink-safe workspace and Codex cwd validation using
  canonical path checks
- ignore superseded poll and retry timers, and stop running issues
  that disappear from refresh results
- align workflow state parsing and SPEC.md with upstream list-only
  tracker states and lowercase-only state normalization
- add regression coverage for path safety, sandbox passthrough,
  retry tokening, missing running issues, and workflow parsing

Rationale:
- upstream Symphony fixed correctness gaps around workspace safety,
  timer coalescing, and refresh reconciliation that we still had
- our workflow contract needs to match the current upstream spec so
  the parser and documentation do not drift
- locking the changes down with targeted tests reduces the chance of
  reintroducing these failures later

Tests:
- npm run check
- npm test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

simplify pipeline call flow

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

test: add worktree AC coverage

Add explicit @worktree-support coverage for shadow command root resolution, serve default .kspec resolution, and initContext single-call git resolution.

Task: @01KKCEDW31WB42ZX52NJA51B4V
Spec: @worktree-support

feat(cli): add plan export command

Add  with stdout, file, and JSON export paths plus CLI coverage for the new plan-export spec and inherited traits.

Task: @01KKCYMCNZ94ZJKP84XYCCBVNM
Spec: @plan-export
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(core): reduce noisy test output

Summary:
- add a root logger console toggle so tests can suppress runtime log
  chatter without changing file logging behavior
- preload a quiet test setup and switch the Node test runner to the
  dot reporter for compact agent-friendly output
- cover the new logger toggle with a unit test

Rationale:
- integration tests were emitting orchestrator and agent runner logs on
  every passing run, which wastes context during coding-agent sessions
- keeping logs opt-in preserves a debugging escape hatch without making
  normal test runs noisy

Tests:
- npm test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(core): add hierarchy-aware issue orchestration

Summary:
- load parent and child hierarchy from Linear and expose it in workflow
  templates and runner prompt rendering
- block Todo parent dispatch while child work or blockers remain active
  and allow ready sibling issues to run in parallel
- persist service logs to tmp/symphony.log, preserve websocket shutdown
  details, and treat post-turn session closes as clean boundaries
- refresh workflow docs, remove old workflow examples, and update the
  Linear skill guidance for GitHub PR auto-linking

Rationale:
- agents need parent and sibling context to execute parent and child
  issues without extra manual tracker inspection
- dispatch should respect hierarchy and dependency state so higher-level
  work does not start before prerequisite child work is terminal
- richer logging and transport detail improve observability for
  unattended runs and align the docs with the current workflow

Tests:
- npm test --workspace @symphony/core
- npm run typecheck --workspace @symphony/core

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(core): harden logger and app-server shutdown

Summary:
- make persistent logger setup best-effort so CLI startup does not fail
  when the default log path cannot be created
- suppress transport-close warnings during intentional app-server
  shutdown and reject in-flight work directly from stop()
- add regression coverage for unwritable log paths and intentional
  session teardown

Rationale:
- persistent logging is optional, so an unwritable cwd should degrade to
  console logging instead of preventing service startup
- successful runs were emitting warning-level transport failures during
  normal teardown, which obscured real transport problems

Tests:
- npm test --workspace @symphony/core
- npm run typecheck --workspace @symphony/core

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: resolve worktree project roots

Add worktree-aware project root resolution and thread projectRoot through context, daemon identity, and serve/shadow defaults.

let CLI.run own process exit

Treat the CLI entry routine as a true command entrypoint: help and version exit directly, parse failures exit 1, and normal completion falls through with implicit success.

commit.message:
refactor(simulator): streamline runtime and ring integration

Summary:
- add a simpler simulator lifecycle with `start/1` and unified
  `stop/1` handling for in-memory and UDP-backed runtimes
- replace the old simulator deep test with ring-shaped integration tests
  for both simulator and real hardware using EK1100, EL1809, and EL2809
- narrow the public simulator slave API toward driver-backed devices and
  align docs/tests with the new ring and hardware-first model

Rationale:
- the previous simulator setup exposed too much internal structure and
  required awkward manual endpoint cleanup in tests
- ring-shaped integration coverage better matches the actual target
  topology and keeps simulator and hardware validation aligned
- driver-backed simulation is the clearer long-term public API than
  profile-style convenience builders

Validation:
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

simplify the OptionParser wiring

Restore the fixed help text output, keep the option definitions inline in CLI.run, and avoid routing parser results through callback lambdas.

This preserves the CLI behavior from the earlier PR while matching the simpler structure requested in review.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
switch the CLI to OptionParser

Replace the ad hoc option loop with Ruby stdlib OptionParser, preserving front-of-command option parsing while adding conventional -V/--version support alongside -v/--verbose.

Keep the existing help content shape, retain --no-jit and atomic-write parsing, and add executable-level coverage for help/version behavior.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(driver): make simulator hooks explicit

Summary:
- make driver identity and simulator hydration explicit callbacks
- promote simulator definitions to a public opaque authored type
- move derived EEPROM and ESC memory hydration into the simulator
- extract EL1809 and EL2809 example drivers into integration support

Rationale:
- reflective function_exported checks made the driver contract harder to
  reason about and diluted the pre-release API
- simulator definitions should expose authored device data without leaking
  derived low-level runtime state

  public driver docs shorter and the examples easier to reuse

Summary:
- rename the public simulator concept from fixtures to devices and
  move the internal builder to `EtherCAT.Simulator.Slave.Definition`
- add cross-device signal wiring through `connect/3`,
  `disconnect/3`, and `connections/1`
- update the simulator docs and integration coverage to match the
  new public API shape

Rationale:
- the simulator is now a real library feature, so fixture-oriented
  test naming no longer fits the public model
- higher-level tools such as a simulator widget need a first-class
  way to compose devices and route signals between them
- keeping the runtime and docs aligned makes the simulator easier to
  understand and extend with new device profiles

commit.message:
fix(bot): prevent callback vote flow from crashing on Telegram API tr… (#17)

* fix(bot): prevent callback vote flow from crashing on Telegram API transport errors

### Motivation

- 由于 Telegram API 偶发网络错误（例如 `ECONNABORTED`）会在处理回调时使 `ctx.answerCbQuery` / `ctx.reply` 抛出未捕获异常并导致 bot 进程崩溃，需要在回调路径和全局更新链上增加容错与可观测性。
- 需要避免空白或仅含空格的环境变量（例如 `DEAL_STORE_PATH`、`RPC_URL`）被误判为有效值从而引发运行时路径或配置错误。

### Description

- 在 `apps/telegram-bot/src/index.ts` 中让 `requiredEnv` 在校验前执行 `trim()`，新增 `optionalEnv` 辅助函数并将 `DEAL_STORE_PATH`、`RPC_URL`、`TELEGRAM_ANNOUNCE_CHAT_ID` 改为通过 `optionalEnv` 解析以把空白视为未设置。
- 在 `apps/telegram-bot/src/index.ts` 增加全局 `bot.catch(...)`，把 update 处理链上的未捕获异常统一记录为 `botUpdateError` 审计日志，避免进程直接退出。
- 在 `apps/telegram-bot/src/commands/dispute.ts` 的投票回调里为 `ctx.answerCbQuery(...)` 和 `ctx.reply(...)` 添加 `try/catch`，在 Telegram API 传输或响应失败时记录 `telegramApiError` 审计事件并吞掉瞬时网络错误以保持 bot 存活。
- 对修改文件运行了格式化并确保 TypeScript 构建通过以保持代码风格与编译约束。

### Testing

- 运行了 `pnpm -s prettier --write apps/telegram-bot/src/index.ts apps/telegram-bot/src/commands/dispute.ts`，文件已格式化成功。
- 运行了 `pnpm --filter @dgp/telegram-bot build`，TypeScript 编译与构建通过。
- 在运行环境中手动走过关键路径（`/deal -> /dispute -> 点击 vote`），在出现 Telegram 传输错误时验证为记录错误日志且 bot 进程未退出（手动验证）。

* fix(bot): centralize transient telegram api error guard for callbacks

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

- 前端新增 features/waapi typed API 与通用 composables，移除页面重复逻辑
- 路由改为单一配置源，导航由路由驱动，按 meta.keepAlive 控制页面缓存
- Rust 将 lib.rs 拆分为 state/waapi_client/commands 模块并抽取公共调用逻辑
- 新增 parse_return_fields 与订阅重复开启相关单测
- 新增 pnpm check、.editorconfig，README 统一为 pnpm 工作流

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

- keep the deep integration suite on the real UDP transport and update

Rationale:
- the simulator is now intended for tooling such as kino_ethercat, so
  it needs a real library boundary instead of test-only support code
- compiling it from lib keeps the API available to consumers while the

- the README and active plan now make the current coverage and missing

commit.message:
feat(test): add simulator fault injection coverage

Summary:
- add simulator faults for dropped responses, wrong WKC, named slave
  disconnects, SAFEOP retreat, AL error latching, and mailbox aborts
- extend deep integration coverage for recovery, shared-domain reconnect,
  mailbox aborts, and slave-local SAFEOP retreat handling
- document the new Milestone 4 runtime and fault-injection behavior in
  the support slave reference docs

Rationale:
- deepen the UDP simulator so it can reproduce real recovery scenarios
  that were previously only observable on hardware
- keep the support slave aligned with the SOES-derived spec notes while
  making the integration test path a stronger regression oracle

Validation:
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: polish shared UI overhaul pass

Major Features:
- Main menu and navigation polish
  - Flattened profile strip presentation, tightened action layout, and added explicit controller focus-neighbor flow
  - Kept update checks click-triggered and maintained difficulty persistence behavior
- Gameplay HUD and result-screen refinement
  - Improved objective/multiplier readability and reduced playfield overlap with denser HUD sizing
  - Refined complete/game-over screens and set/level selection layouts for consistent shared-theme density
- Leaderboards and menu consistency
  - Reworked high scores structure with reusable score rows and challenge-aware tabs
  - Unified settings/stats/keybindings/set-select/level-select visuals under shared theme tokens

Code Improvements:

commit.message:
feat(test): extend UDP slave simulator coverage

Summary:
- add richer support fixtures for coupler and LAN9252-style I/O rings
- add mailbox-capable simulator behavior with expedited CoE upload and
  download handling in PREOP
- extend deep integration coverage to heterogeneous rings and public
  SDO roundtrips over the real UDP transport
- update the simulator README and derived SOES notes to match the
  implemented Milestone 2 and Milestone 3 scope

Rationale:
- the simulator should cover more than the minimal one-byte digital I/O
  happy path before mailbox work begins
- deep tests need a protocol-faithful mailbox path so PREOP diagnostics
  and configuration can be exercised without hardware
- keeping the support docs aligned with the derived slave spec makes the
  simulator easier to evolve without drifting from its test-only role

commit.message:
feat(test): add UDP slave simulator for deep tests

Summary:
- add compiled test support for simulated EtherCAT slaves and a
  multi-slave simulator behind a real UDP endpoint
- add deep integration tests that boot the normal master over the
  UDP transport and exchange cyclic I/O against one and two
  simulated slaves
- tighten UDP startup validation, ignore the vendored SOES tree,
  and document the simulator design against derived slave spec notes

Rationale:
- deep integration coverage needs a protocol-faithful peer that can
  exercise normal master startup and cyclic I/O without hardware
- loopback UDP keeps the test path end-to-end through the real bus
  and transport layers without requiring root privileges
- stricter AL transition handling and explicit slave spec notes make
  the simulator a stronger regression oracle instead of a happy-path
  echo fixture

Validation:
- mix format test/ethercat/deep_integration_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/deep_integration_test.exs
- mix test
- mix docs

commit.message:
fix(api): harden timeout contracts and dev release docs

Summary:
- bound plain Master API calls with a local call timeout and return
  {:error, :timeout} instead of exiting callers
- update public specs/docs for master-facing calls and subscribe/3 to
  reflect timeout behavior accurately
- clarify the 0.3.0-dev development line in README and CHANGELOG while
  keeping the latest Hex install snippet at 0.2.0
- add a regression test for EtherCAT.state/0 timing out cleanly when the
  master is suspended

Rationale:
- the await APIs already translated local call timeouts, but the rest of
  the public Master surface still used an unbounded call and could hang
  or exit callers under a wedged local master
- the public docs and types should match real timeout behavior so users
  and Dialyzer see the same contract
- the repo should clearly distinguish unreleased 0.3.0-dev work on main
  from the latest published Hex release

Validation:
- mix format lib/ethercat/master/api.ex lib/ethercat.ex README.md CHANGELOG.md test/ethercat_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat_test.exs
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Fix nested list continuation indent

Use a shared helper for list-item continuation indentation so
paragraph wrapping and block serialization agree inside definition
list details. This fixes a false positive where nested ordered list
continuation lines lost one leading space on reformat, causing
`--check` and `--diff` to report already formatted files as dirty.

Add a minimal regression test for the nested ordered-list case and
record the fix in CHANGES.md.

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Propagate runParser callback exceptions

Remove the try/catch-based callback arity detection in runParser's
help, version, completion, and error paths. Callbacks now always
receive the numeric exit code, and real exceptions propagate instead
of being swallowed by a zero-argument fallback.

Update the facade regression tests to assert exception propagation
and record the behavior change in CHANGES.md.

Fixes https://github.com/dahlia/optique/issues/153

commit.message:
fix(bus): gate domain recovery on carrier state

Summary:
- add carrier-aware bus runtime info and expose link monitor mode in
  diagnostics
- stop domains immediately on confirmed :down and block stopped-domain
  restarts while carrier is absent
- replace the bus recovery test seam with a dedicated non-async test
  that registers a fake EtherCAT.Bus

Rationale:
- the previous recovery gating used the bus scheduler state, which let
  stopped domains churn restart->timeout->stop while the cable was
  physically unplugged
- recovery should follow actual carrier availability, and the runtime
  model should stay honest instead of pretending the bus server is
  configurable

Validation:
- mix test
- mix docs
- real hardware unplug/replug on enp0s31f6

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Skip unavailable agents in each iteration

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
refactor(bus): remove vintage_net dependency

Summary:
- remove the vintage_net dependency and its passive config
- add internal bus interface and link-monitor helpers based on
  sysfs and netlink, with polling fallback
- update raw socket transport, redundant link handling, examples,
  and tests to use the new bus-owned monitoring path

Rationale:
- keep link detection inside the bus subsystem instead of leaking a
  network-management dependency through transport and link code
- preserve link change notifications while keeping the library
  dependency-free at runtime

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test
- mix docs
- mix run examples/diag.exs --interface enp0s31f6
- mix run examples/probe.exs --interface enp0s31f6 --frames 2 --timeout_ms 200

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(runtime): clarify state-machine boundaries

Summary:
- update the maintainer and public docs to describe Master, Slave,
  Domain, and DC as state-machine modules with helper namespaces
- fix stale navigation and architecture references, including the
  ESC SII path and source-adjacent briefing entry points
- tighten the Domain and DC diagrams so they no longer imply extra
  gen_statem states that only exist as runtime data

Rationale:
- the recent refactors made the runtime shells much easier to audit
  against the EtherCAT model, but the docs still used vague "shell"
  wording and left several boundaries implicit
- clearer state-machine terminology makes the design easier to review
  without relying on project-specific jargon

Validation:
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(readme): add codex tui notification config

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(master): move slave recovery mechanics out of shell

Summary:
- move reconnect authorization and PREOP-to-OP retry mechanics from
  EtherCAT.Master into EtherCAT.Master.Recovery
- drop direct Slave.API usage from the master state-machine shell
- keep master state clauses focused on event routing and transitions

Rationale:
- the shell should mention slave/domain/dc events as state-machine
  concepts, but it should not perform low-level subsystem operations
- moving the mechanics into Recovery makes the master easier to audit
  against the protocol lifecycle and removes duplicated reconnect logic

Validation:
- mix format lib/ethercat/master.ex lib/ethercat/master/recovery.ex
- mix test test/ethercat/master_test.exs
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: batch DB writes, async blob pipeline, and traceable scan logs

- add per-scan trace id across scan and blob logs\n- decouple data phase from blob writes in cron flow\n- enforce blob concurrency limit/alert (10/8)\n- batch c2c/product/snapshot writes and single-request blob update\n- bump frontend version to 0.9.5.4 and update docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
